user_query:
I'm meeting my advisor next week about my thesis on the economic impacts of post-Civil War industrialization in the South. I need a focused, verifiable risk assessment of potential source bias and reliability issues, plus concrete action items. Please do the following using the provided local files and new outputs under outputs/:

Inputs:
- input/source_list.csv (my planned sources)
- input/methodology.md (my current methodology draft)

Required deliverables (create all):
1) outputs/web/findings.json
- Purpose: Document 3–5 official guidance resources that discuss pitfalls, biases, or limitations when using late-19th-century U.S. economic/historical sources (roughly 1865–1900) and their derivatives.
- How to gather: Perform search engine queries to find resources from reputable institutions (e.g., U.S. Census Bureau, Library of Congress, National Archives, ICPSR, or major university libraries). Avoid blogs, wikis, or non-official commentary. Do not include full URLs.
- JSON array of objects with fields:
  - id: string like "S1", "S2", ... (unique)
  - organization: string (issuing institution)
  - resource_title: string
  - resource_type: one of ["research guide", "documentation", "dataset user note", "archival guide"]
  - publication_year: integer or null if not stated
  - key_takeaways: array of 3–5 brief strings stating concrete risk considerations (e.g., undercounting, geographic coverage gaps, survivorship bias, OCR errors, definitional changes)
  - citation_hint: short string (e.g., Organization. Title. Year if available.)
  - access_descriptor: short descriptor of the official site or collection name or domain pattern only (e.g., "census.gov historical publications", "loc.gov Freedmen's Bureau collection") — no direct URLs

2) outputs/methodology_with_risk.md
- Start from input/methodology.md and append a new section titled "Risk Assessment". Keep the original text; do not delete it. The new section must include:
  - A brief overview paragraph tailored to my topic.
  - A "Risk themes" list defining 3–5 themes with stable codes [RT1], [RT2], ... Each theme must have 1–2 sentences and be grounded in the takeaways from findings.json by citing at least one [S#] id per theme (e.g., "[RT2] ... see [S1], [S3]").
  - A "Mitigation plan" subsection that maps theme codes to concrete steps I can take in my workflow, and references specific source ids from input/source_list.csv (by their id field) where appropriate.

3) outputs/assessed_sources.csv
- Create a new CSV using all rows from input/source_list.csv and add these columns:
  - risk_level: one of Low, Medium, High (justify via theme selection and evidence refs)
  - risk_theme_ids: delimited list of theme codes from outputs/methodology_with_risk.md (e.g., RT1;RT3)
  - mitigations: 1–2 terse, concrete steps tailored to that source
  - evidence_refs: delimited list of S# ids from outputs/web/findings.json that support the assessment of that source
- Ensure every risk_theme_ids value references codes actually defined in outputs/methodology_with_risk.md, and every evidence_refs value references ids present in outputs/web/findings.json.

4) outputs/meeting_notes.md
- Prepare meeting-ready notes with the following headings and content:
  - "Meeting Goal" (1–2 sentences)
  - "Key risks to discuss" (bullet list referencing [RT#] codes)
  - "Decisions needed" (3–5 bullets)
  - "Action items" (5–8 items), each item must include: item_id (A1, A2, ...), related_source_id(s) from input/source_list.csv, related risk_theme_ids, a concise task description, owner set to "me", and a brief dependency/note (e.g., "align definitions per [S2]").

Additional constraints:
- Use a search engine to find the official guidance; do not include direct URLs in any output. Identify resources by organization, title, and access_descriptor only.
- Keep outputs consistent across files: [S#] ids in findings.json must match citations in methodology_with_risk.md and evidence_refs in assessed_sources.csv; [RT#] codes must match between methodology_with_risk.md, assessed_sources.csv, and meeting_notes.md.
- Do not invent facts about my local files; base all source-specific assessments on input/source_list.csv and the generalized risks drawn from findings.json.

Deliver exactly the four outputs at the specified paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
